description_long: This is a medium-sized, weathered leather bucket or barrel with a pentagonal lid and a rugged, aged surface. The main body is constructed from brown, distressed leather panels, stitched together with visible seams and reinforced edges. A looped leather handle is attached to the front, suggesting it can be carried or hung. The lid appears to be a flap that folds over the top, secured by a stitched patch, and the overall form is slightly tapered with a flat base. The sides show the continuation of the leather material, with the handle structure extending down both sides. The object exudes a rustic, utilitarian aesthetic, suitable for carrying or storing items in an urban or outdoor setting.
description: A weathered brown leather bucket with a pentagonal lid, stitched seams, and a looped leather handle, exuding a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main leather body, stitched seams, and the two side straps extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the leather, the angular lid, and the profile of the handle structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the pentagonal lid flap, stitched patch, and the looped leather handle attached to the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same leather construction and handle structure as the other side.
